name: INR/USD Exchange Rate
summary: Day 84 of Hormuz blockade (Friday May 22). **USD/INR ₹95.69 CLOSE — RETRACED FROM INTRA-WEEK RECORD LOW ₹97.15 ON ~$5B RBI INTERVENTION IN 48 HOURS. THE REVERSAL IS INTERVENTION-DRIVEN, NOT FLOW-DRIVEN. SUSTAINABILITY QUESTION IS NOW OPERATIONAL, NOT THEORETICAL.** **Primary chain (~95% RBI intervention)**: RBI sold ~$2-3B Thursday + ~$2-3B Friday through large state-run banks = **~$5B in 48 hours** (Business Standard, Bloomberg estimates) — multiples of May 9-15 weekly $7.79B drawdown rate. **RBI separately announced a $5B dollar-rupee buy/sell swap auction for May 26 to boost rupee liquidity** (Business Standard May 22) — structural FX-liquidity-management tool stacked on spot intervention. **Forex reserves print landed Friday**: $688.9B for week ended May 15, **DOWN $8B from $696.9B** (Akashvani News primary, RBI weekly statistical supplement) — confirms prior week's intervention; next week's print (landing May 30 for week ended May 22) will capture this week's ~$5B intervention. **Counter-vector (~5% organic)**: FII flows continuing — May 20 alone net sell -₹1,597 crore; May MTD net sales now ₹25,896 crore (Groww/NSE primary, BSE secondary) — pace flat versus May 19 brief's ₹27,048 crore reading. **Structural bear unchanged**: FII YTD CY26 outflow tracking ~₹2 lakh cr; FPI ownership ~16% (15-year low). **Camp B framing nuance** (per intel A3): on the kinetic axis, INR retraced (geopolitical relief read), but FII outflows continued, reserves bled, structural bear holds. **The May 26 swap auction is the regime signal** — RBI moving from purely spot intervention to structural FX-liquidity-management architecture; what a central bank does when it wants to preserve reserves while keeping the currency capped. **Sustainability now operational**: at ~$5B per major weekly episode, RBI has ~$150B deployable before import cover falls to 2013 levels = ~30 weeks of buffer at this pace; in stress accelerations, ~8-12 weeks. **The ₹96 trigger remains `active_fired` from May 18** but trajectory has split between intervention-driven retracement and structural pressure. **Iranian Rial Day 5 of 5 (last Bonbast May 15 1,822K)**: trigger likely resolved UNTRIPPED (not separately re-verified this search window). 2M trigger ETA pushed past mid-June. **Reversibility**: If Pakistan deal signs + Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions + FII flows turn positive → INR retraces toward ₹93-94 organically (intervention can stop). If Pakistan deal fails + Brent re-spikes toward $115-120 → ₹97-98 path opens despite intervention. **New trigger added**: 'RBI reserves print May 30 (week ending May 22) shows $5B+ drawdown' — confirms structural intervention rate. PRIOR (May 19 — Monday): **₹96 TRIGGER FIRES — USD/INR ₹96.28-96.29 ALL-TIME LOW MAY 18 (+0.6% 1D, +3.5% 1M, +6.3% 3M)**. The May 12-added 'USD/INR breaks ₹96 within 5-7 sessions' trigger BREACHES on Day 6 of 5-7 window — within window. Trigger moves watching → ACTIVE_FIRED. Conversion: rupee depreciation moves from 'watching' risk to active RBI-direct-intervention binary. RBI must now choose: (a) defend ₹96 with sustained dollar sales (accelerates reserves drawdown; May 1 week was -$7.79B to $690.69B, partially offset by May 8 week +$6.295B to $696.99B forward-book hedging); (b) capitulate to ₹97-98 next pivot; (c) administrative measures (May 13 gold/silver duty hike 6%→15%, weekend silver licensed-import regime, April 10 ₹100M NOP cap). **Sonal Varma (Nomura) 'INR through ₹97 inside 30 days even without further escalation' call now operationally validated.** Drivers: Brent $111.47 (+2.0%, StanChart $130-145 path activates same day) → India imports ~85% of crude → every $1 above ~$83 baseline adds ~$1.6-2B/year to import bill → at $111, incremental CAD drain ~$45-55B annualised vs pre-crisis. FII outflow ₹2 lakh cr YTD CY26 (NSDL primary) exceeds full-year 2025 (₹1.66 lakh cr) by ~₹40k cr; FPI ownership ~16% (15-year low, below DII for first time ever). Reuters ranks rupee worst-performing Asian currency in 2026. **Sensex -1.30% / Nifty -1.04% Monday open** confirms cascade self-reinforcing. **Reversibility ASYMMETRIC**: crisis component (~85%) reverses ONLY on (a) Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions AND (b) FII flow positive — neither imminent. RBI ~$150B deployable before import cover falls to 2013 levels = meaningful buffer, but curve steepening. **A7 IRANIAN RIAL ALANCHAND/BONBAST Day 4 of 5 — DIVERGENCE NARROWING NOT WIDENING**: Alanchand 1,803,000 IRR/USD May 18 (page implies Sunday close 1,793K — conflicts with yesterday's logged 1,819K); Bonbast last May 15 print 1,822K. Two readings (Alanchand reset baseline OR quote refresh) both point to convergence, not divergence. Trigger 'source divergence persists beyond 5 sessions' likely to resolve UNTRIPPED tomorrow. **Day 79 of Hormuz blockade (Sunday). MARKETS CLOSED SUNDAY — OPERATIVE PRICES ARE FRIDAY MAY 15 CLOSES**: USD/INR ₹95.96 (+0.3% 1D, +3.1% 1M, +5.9% 3M) — fourth straight record close; intraday high ₹95.9825 = $0.018 (~1.75 paise) from breaching the ₹96 Varma trigger. **The Varma ₹96 trigger continues at Day 4 of 5-7 window — intraday breach, no closing breach yet. Monday May 18 close is the operative test.** **A4/A5 IRANIAN RIAL — BONBAST vs ALANCHAND SOURCE DIVERGENCE DAY 3 OF 5**: Alanchand USD-HAV (Hawala/remittance) at **1,819,000 IRR/USD May 17** (-0.16% from May 16's 1,822K — slight strengthening). Bonbast directional read May 15 was 1,822,000 (+0.61% reversal). **Net read**: Iranian free-market rial is in a 1,810–1,822K trading range — neutral-to-mildly-weakening on Bonbast, neutral-to-mildly-strengthening on Alanchand Hawala. The series divergence is now Day 3 of 5; if it continues two more sessions, the methodology re-anchor decision becomes binding. The 2,000,000 hyperinflation-trigger ETA continues to push out — at current pace, linear extrapolation no longer reaches 2M before mid-June. **The directional read is now BIFURCATED** and reflects bona fide market uncertainty about whether (a) Iran is heading toward kinetic escalation that would gap the rial weaker, or (b) Pakistan-mediated diplomacy + selective Chinese transit + Saudi pact proposal price in as easing. Both narratives have material weight today. **RBI weekly reserves print delayed from May 16 — will land Monday-Tuesday; if >$10B drawdown, sustainability question moves from theoretical to operational.** **PRIOR (May 16):** USD/INR ₹95.96 May 15 close (+0.3% 1D, +3.1% 1M, +5.9% 3M); intraday high ₹95.9825 = $0.018 (~1.75 paise) from breaching the ₹96 Varma trigger (Day 4 of the 5-7 session window)** — pair did not close at the high; rupee under 'strong selling pressure' (Trading Pedia May 15 primary). **DXY at 99.27 (+0.4% 1D, high since two weeks)** and WTI near $106 on Hormuz strain were the upward drivers. RBI direct intervention near ₹95.96 is the operational defence — same zone as yesterday's brief. **The Varma trigger therefore remains 'Day 4 of 5-7 window, intraday breach, no closing breach yet.'** **A9 RBI FOREX RESERVES — most recent data is week ending May 8 = $696.99B (+$6.3B w/w recovery from May 1 $690.69B)**; May 15-ending print not yet released. The 'anything above $10B this week moves the sustainability question from theoretical to operational' specification remains a pending test rather than satisfied test. Brent +1.6% over the summit period and the USD/INR intraday high of 95.9825 on May 15 (close to ₹96 trigger) suggest the relief was indeed temporary. **A5 IRANIAN RIAL BONBAST May 15 = 1,822,000 IRR/USD (+0.61% from May 14), REVERSING the 3-day Alanchand strengthening pattern**; the two series have diverged on May 15. Architecture trigger baseline is Bonbast free-market — directional read flips from 'gradually pricing summit-pause' to 'neutral-to-mildly-weakening pending operational outcome.' At Bonbast pace, 2M ETA still does not reach 2M before mid-June. Source-divergence is itself the signal. **Tomorrow needs reconciliation**; the brief should describe today's Bonbast print without claiming continued strengthening. **Three-force stagflation collision on May 15 reset US rates regime**: April PPI +1.4% + Warsh effective May 16 + Trump-Iran reversal → 10Y +14bp to 4.595% / 30Y +11bp to 5.121% (Bloomberg 'worst weekly Treasury rout in a year'); DXY +0.4% to 99.27; gold -2.6%; silver -9.1%. **Reversibility ASYMMETRIC: crisis component (~85%) reverses ONLY on (a) Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions AND (b) FII flow positive — neither imminent.** PRIOR (May 15): **C7 USD/INR ₹95.90 — FOURTH STRAIGHT RECORD CLOSE** (+0.2% 1D, +2.7% 1M, +5.9% 3M vs pre-war ~₹87.92). **FPI ownership now ~16% — lowest in 15-20 years AND below DII for the first time ever; $19B of $2 lakh crore CY26 outflow since Iran war started — flow is overwhelmingly crisis-linked.** Primary chain (~60% FII-flow): FII outflows ₹2 lakh crore exited Indian equities in first 5 months of 2026, surpassing all of 2025's ₹1.66 lakh crore; foreign safe-haven USD bid on Trump Iran ultimatum amplifies pressure. Secondary chain (~25% oil-import bill, crisis): Brent $107.42 → India imports ~85% of oil → CAD widening; Outlook Business: rupee under 'mounting pressure from surging oil prices tied to West Asia conflict'; RBI controlling speed not direction — selling dollars near ₹95.96 intervention threshold; **Modi UAE LPG/SPR signaling reads as confirmation of energy-supply anxiety, not relief**. Tertiary chain (~15% non-crisis structural): RBI March 27 $100M cap on banks' Net Open Position INR (compliance April 10); April 1 prohibition on AD banks offering NDF contracts to corporates (partial relaxation April 20); NDF maturity timing is mechanical/independent — recurring pressure points; FCNR(B) swap window opened at fixed 3.5% for 3yr+ deposits; NRI dollar deposit scheme (2013 playbook, $26B then) under active study but no decision yet. **Sonal Varma's ₹96-within-5-7-sessions trigger is Day 3 of window. RBI weekly reserves print due May 16 is the next sustainability test.** Government considering bond-tax cut for foreign investors — if announced, partial relief possible. **A5 IRANIAN RIAL THIRD CONSECUTIVE SESSION OF SLIGHT STRENGTHENING ON HAWALA/REMITTANCE SERIES**: 1,817K (May 13) → 1,811K (May 14) → 1,810K (May 15), -0.39% from May 12 close 1,842K. 2M-trigger ETA continues to push out — at new pace no longer reaches 2M before mid-June. Bonbast direct fetch failed today; tomorrow re-attempt. **DXY 99.13 (+0.3% 1D)**; EUR/USD 1.16 (-0.6%); USD/CNY 6.80 (+0.2%, PBOC fix 6.8415 vs Reuters estimate 6.7976 = weaker-than-expected fix signaling Beijing tolerance for further yuan depreciation); USD/KRW ₩1,500.68 (+0.7% — FIRST BREACH OF 1,500 PSYCHOLOGICAL LEVEL); GBP/USD 1.34 (-1.2%); USD/JPY ¥158.44 (+0.4%); USD/BRL R$4.98 (-0.8%); USD/TRY ₺45.54 (+0.2%). **C7 USD/INR ₹95.74 MAY 14 MORNING — RECORD CONTINUES, PACE SLOWED, ₹96 TRIGGER DAY 2 OF 5-7 WINDOW**. May 13 close ₹95.31 (4th straight session of weakness, fresh closing record per Indian press; intraday range ~₹95.45-95.75); RBI intervention sustaining defence at ₹95.50-75 zone. **Pace of weakening MATERIALLY SLOWED from May 11-12 collapse** — RBI active dollar-sale intervention is the operative defensive tool above ₹95.50. The 'USD/INR breaks ₹96 within 5-7 sessions' trigger is at Day 2 of 5-7 with ₹96 not yet breached; at current ~5-7bps/day pace, ₹96 hits May 21-23 if Brent doesn't retrace. RBI weekly reserves print due ~May 16 (Friday) — last week was -$7.79bn; if this week extends above $10bn, intervention sustainability moves from theoretical to operational concern. **A5 IRANIAN RIAL REVERSED MAY 13 — 1,842K (May 12) → 1,817K (May 13) Hawala/Remittance series = -1.36% over 24h** (Alanchand primary). FIRST material reversal since May 6-10 stabilisation. 2M trigger ETA pushed beyond May 22-24 — at new pace a linear extrapolation no longer reaches 2M before late-May/early-June. **Caveat: Remittance ≠ Bonbast — tomorrow's pipeline re-anchor on Bonbast for trigger calibration.** **DXY 98.48 (-0.0% 1D) — DOLLAR FAILING TO BID ON CRISIS DAY** (G5): crisis days usually bid USD on safe-haven flow; -0.0% 1D, only +0.4% 1M. Currency pressure on EM (INR ₹95.74, BRL R$4.98) is driven by domestic stories + commodity-import pass-through, NOT global USD bid. USD/CNY 6.78 (-0.1%, PBOC stability into summit); USD/KRW ₩1,489.81 (-0.2% 1D) won steadying as KOSPI rally absorbs FX outflow; USD/BRL R$4.98 (+1.5% 1D) worst EM-FX (Bolsonaro-Banco Master scandal); USD/JPY ¥157.83 (+0.1%); USD/TRY ₺45.43 (+0.1%, +4.0% 3M). Sonal Varma (Nomura) call validated session-by-session: pace slowed (May 13 modest vs May 11-12 collapse) consistent with RBI active intervention; 'INR through ₹97 inside 30 days even without further escalation' call remains cleanest forward test. **Reversibility ASYMMETRIC: crisis component (~85%) reverses ONLY on (a) Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions AND (b) FII flow turn positive — neither imminent. RBI economists note India can deploy ~$150bn before import cover falls to 2013 levels = meaningful buffer.** PRIOR (May 13): **USD/INR FRESH CLOSING RECORD ₹95.63 MAY 12 (vs ₹95.54 May 12 intraday in yesterday's brief); intraday hovering ₹95.58 May 13; ₹95.50 RBI DIRECT-INTERVENTION THRESHOLD NOW BROKEN IN CLOSE PRINTS, not just intraday. Markets dossier Section A logs ₹95.68 May 13 morning print = continuation but PACE SLOWED from yesterday's +1.2% to +0.3% / +5bps.** Moody's CUT FY26 GDP 0.8pp to 6.0% (from 6.8%) = SOVEREIGN-RATING actor in same direction. Hardeep Puri publicly quantified OMC under-recovery at ₹1,000 cr/day, total on track ₹1.98 lakh cr — fiscal-deficit channel quantified. RBI reserves drawdown next print due ~May 16. Nifty +0.5% intraday relief bounce of +117 pts after May 12 -436-pt collapse = TECHNICAL not fundamental; cumulative drawdown still -1.83% from May 11 peak. **A5 RIAL RECALIBRATED — May 12 close print is 1,842,000 IRR/USD (+0.66% d/d, +12,000 IRR); yesterday's brief listed 1,853K but that was likely intraday quote; revised 48h sequence 1,799K → 1,830K (May 10) → 1,842K (May 12) = +2.4% over 72 hours (vs prior brief's +3.0% over 48 hours). 2M trigger ETA shifts modestly to May 22-24 if linear (vs May 19-21 prior). Canary still accelerating, slightly less aggressively than implied.** DXY 98.37 (+0.1% 1D); USD/CNY 6.79 (-0.1%, PBOC stability into Trump-Xi summit); USD/KRW ₩1,489.12 (+1.0% 1D = disconnect — currency says capital outflow, equity says relief on FM Cho 'too early' HMM Namu hedge). **NEXT TEST ₹96 WITHIN 5-7 SESSIONS if Brent doesn't retrace; trigger 'USD/INR breaks ₹96' now Day 1 of 5-7 window.** Sonal Varma (Nomura) structural call validated TWICE — May 12 ₹95.54 intraday + May 12 ₹95.63 close. Reversibility ASYMMETRIC: crisis component (~85%) reverses ONLY on (a) Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions AND (b) FII flow turn positive — neither imminent. **PRIOR-DAY CONTEXT (May 12):** Trigger 'USD/INR crosses ₹95/USD' RE-BREACHED with fresh high after May 6 reversal — RBI's macro-prudential toolkit (NOP cap + NDF restriction operational since May 5) slowed but did not stop the move. May 11 hit ₹95.0930 (breach of ₹95) intraday low ₹95.32 record; May 12 ₹95.54 = fresh record. **Cascade was self-reinforcing: Brent $105.58 (+1.3% 1D) → CAD widening → FII single-day net sell ₹8,437.56 cr May 11 (highest since April 24) → DII absorbed ~70% (₹5,939.65 cr) → YTD CY26 FII outflow ₹2.06 lakh cr (+₹14k cr in 2 sessions) already exceeds full-year 2025 by ₹40k cr → RBI forex reserves -$7.79bn to $690.69bn week ended May 1 (sharp drawdown, evidence active intervention; reserves down ~$38bn from late-Feb peak ~$728.49bn).** India 10Y G-Sec 6.96-6.99%; new 10Y auction cut-off near 6.95% (auction-anchored despite crude bid). DXY 98.13 (+0.2% 1D); EUR/USD 1.18 (-0.1% 1D); USD/CNY 6.79 (-0.1% 1D); broad USD slight strength but not the driver. USD/KRW ₩1,488.28 +1.9% 1D on HMM Namu Korea-flagged vessel attack + KOSPI -1.7% capital outflow. **IRANIAN RIAL ACCELERATING APPROACH TO 2M TRIGGER:** May 10 1,799K → May 11 1,830K (+1.7%) → **May 12 1,853K (+1.26% d/d, +3.0% over 48h)**; official rate pegged 1,311,203 = ~41% gap; at current pace 2M trigger would hit by May 19-21 if linear (CONFIRMED — AlanChand + Bonbast Persian-calendar 1405/02/22 metadata cross-check). **PM MODI HYDERABAD ADDRESS MAY 10-11: seven appeals incl. defer gold purchases for 1 year + defer foreign travel for 1 year + cut fertilizer 50% + reduce edible oil + reduce fuel consumption + swadeshi push + consumption restraint** = first major Indian-government public engagement of cycle targeting FX pressure points (gold imports ~$50bn/yr drain + crude import bill). FY27 GDP downgrade by global brokerage to 6.2% from 6.7% — 'historically large energy shock with asymmetric macro risks.' BMI pre-war forecast of ₹95-by-year-end being pulled forward 7 months. **The ₹95.50 break invites the next test: ₹96 within 5-7 sessions if Brent doesn't retrace.** Reversibility ASYMMETRIC: crisis component (~85%) reverses ONLY on (a) Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions AND (b) FII flow turn positive. Neither imminent.

current.price: INR ₹96.28-96.29 May 18 ALL-TIME LOW (₹96 trigger BREACHED on Day 6 of 5-7 window; +0.6% 1D); IRR 1,803,000 Alanchand May 18 / 1,822,000 Bonbast last May 15 print (divergence Day 4 of 5, narrowing)
current.delta_1d: INR +0.6% May 18 (₹96 trigger fires); IRR Alanchand series implies -0.88% or +0.56% depending on baseline interpretation — both point to convergence with Bonbast
current.delta_3m: INR +6.3% (₹96.29 vs ₹87.92 pre-crisis ~Feb 28); IRR ~+200% cycle depreciation vs ~625K pre-Feb 28
current.regime: **₹95.50 RBI DIRECT-INTERVENTION THRESHOLD NOW BROKEN IN CLOSE PRINTS — ₹95.63 May 12 close, ₹95.68 May 13 morning. Regime change confirmed.** ₹96 within 5-7 sessions is operative trigger if Brent doesn't retrace. Cascade self-reinforcing: Brent $105.58 → CAD widening → FII -₹8,437 cr single day → ₹2.06 lakh cr YTD → RBI reserves -$7.79bn week ended May 1 to $690.69bn (down ~$38bn from late-Feb peak). RBI NOP cap + NDF restriction operational since May 5 — slowed but did not stop the move. Next test ₹96 within 5-7 sessions if Brent doesn't retrace. **Iranian rial ACCELERATING APPROACH to 2M trigger: 1,853,000 IRR/USD May 12 (+3% over 48h); at current +1-1.5%/day pace, 2M would hit by May 19-21 if linear.** Trump rejection of Iran counter-proposal is proximate driver; new OFAC sanctions tranche (May 11-12, 12 entities + 3 individuals) compounds. Official-vs-free rial gap widening to ~41%.

RE-BREACHED May 11 ₹95.0930 (intraday low ₹95.32 record) on Trump rejection of Iran counter-proposal + FII -₹8,437 cr single-day sell. May 12 ₹95.54 fresh record. ₹95.50 RBI direct-intervention threshold CLEANLY BROKEN.

active_triggers.activation_note: ₹95.54 May 12 intraday — cleanly breaks ₹95.50 RBI direct-intervention threshold. RBI reserves -$7.79bn week ended May 1 = active intervention not stopping the move. Toolkit (NOP cap + NDF restriction) tested and broken.

active_triggers.fire_note: ₹96.28-96.29 all-time low printed Monday May 18 on Day 6 of 5-7 window — within window. Trigger fires on Brent $111.47 (+2.0%, 2-session-sustain rule met) + FII outflow ₹2 lakh cr YTD + Sensex/Nifty open -1.30%/-1.04% + Saudi drone interception + Trump-Netanyahu coordination. **RBI binary now operational**: (a) defend ₹96 with sustained sales (accelerates reserves drawdown; May 1 was -$7.79B, May 8 +$6.295B forward-book hedging not spot intervention); (b) capitulate to ₹97-98 next pivot; (c) administrative (gold/silver duty 6→15% May 13, silver licensed-import weekend, NRI dollar deposit scheme under study). Sonal Varma's '₹97 inside 30 days even without further escalation' call now operationally validated. **Reversibility ASYMMETRIC**: crisis component (~85%) reverses ONLY on (a) Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions AND (b) FII flow positive — neither imminent.

active_triggers.condition: USD/INR strengthens below ₹93 AND Brent below $103 sustained 3 sessions (Branch 3 stabilisation confirmation)
RBI OMC special-credit-facility wound down (Branch 3 sub-$103 sustained 2-3 sessions)

recent_signals.headline: **USD/INR ₹96.44 — FRESH RECORD LOW DESPITE TRUMP POSTPONEMENT (+0.5% 1D); CAMP B SIGNAL — STRUCTURAL DRIVERS OVERRIDE GEOPOLITICAL RELIEF.** Rupee weakened 0.5% to fresh record low on the SAME news that took Brent down 1.6%. **If the postponement were genuine de-escalation, rupee would have rallied 0.3-0.5%; instead it weakened to a fresh record low.** Read: structural drivers (FII outflow + crude premium + UST yields) override geopolitical relief. **Primary chain**: FPIs pulled Rs 27,048 crore from Indian equities MTD May (Business Standard) → 2026 YTD outflows ₹2.2 trillion already EXCEED entire 2025 (₹1.66 trillion) → FII ownership at ~16%, lowest in nearly two decades, now BELOW DII at 17.1% → structural rotation → rupee absorbs the FX impact → crude at $110.32 still 47-50% above $73 baseline → import-bill structural-weakening of CAD → US 30Y at ~5.13% (10-month high) → dollar competitive return + UST yields up. **Secondary**: RBI defense compressed by reserves drawdown ($7.79B week ending May 1; rebuilt $6.3B week ending May 8; next print due Friday May 22). RBI may be choosing NOT to defend ₹96 hard during postponement window to preserve dry powder. **Tuesday cascade**: India raised petrol/diesel by ~90 paise — second hike in a week → CPI direct impact ~15 bps + indirect transport/logistics ~30-50 bps. April CPI 3.48% already up from 3.40%; WPI 8.3% (42-month high). **The April 27 fuel-pass-through trigger now FIRES inside its 30-day window — moves from `watching` to `active_fired`.** **None of the three Indian deployment conditions (Brent <$95, FII flows net positive, INR retracement to ₹93) is met. All three moving the wrong direction.**
sources: Republic World (₹1.92 lakh crore through first week May)
Intel dossier 2026-05-19 A4; Markets dossier C6
recent_signals.verification: USD/INR ₹96.44 fresh record = CONFIRMED operational tape; FII outflows ₹2.2 trillion YTD = CONFIRMED (Business Standard + Republic World); fuel hike ~90 paise = CONFIRMED ACTION (WION + Goodreturns); April 27 fuel-pass-through trigger fires = CONFIRMED inside 30-day window.

recent_signals.headline: **USD/INR ₹96.28-96.29 ALL-TIME LOW MAY 18 — ₹96 TRIGGER FIRES ON DAY 6 OF 5-7 WINDOW (+0.6% 1D, +3.5% 1M, +6.3% 3M).** The May 12-added 'USD/INR breaks ₹96 within 5-7 sessions' trigger BREACHES — within window. Status: watching → ACTIVE_FIRED. **Conversion**: rupee depreciation moves from 'watching' to active RBI-direct-intervention binary. RBI choice: (a) defend ₹96 with sustained dollar sales (accelerates reserves drawdown; May 1 was -$7.79B to $690.69B, May 8 +$6.295B to $696.99B = forward-book hedging, not spot intervention); (b) capitulate to ₹97-98 next pivot; (c) administrative (May 13 gold/silver duty 6%→15% with IGST >18% effective; weekend silver licensed-import regime; April 10 ₹100M NOP cap; April 1 NDF prohibition; FCNR(B) 3.5% swap window). Cumulative carry-cost of RBI defense = Bank Nifty -13.8% 3M (₹3,000-4,000 cr Q4 MTM losses on bank NDF books). **Drivers**: Brent $111.47 (+2.0%, StanChart $130-145 path activates same day — 2-session sustain rule met) → India imports ~85% crude → every $1 above ~$83 baseline adds ~$1.6-2B/yr to import bill → at $111, incremental CAD drain ~$45-55B annualised. FII outflow ₹2 lakh cr YTD CY26 (NSDL primary) exceeds full-year 2025 by ~₹40k cr; FPI ownership ~16% (15-year low, below DII for first time ever). Reuters: rupee worst-performing Asian currency in 2026. Sensex/Nifty open -1.30%/-1.04% Monday. **Sonal Varma (Nomura) '₹97 inside 30 days' call now operationally validated**. **A7 IRANIAN RIAL — DIVERGENCE NARROWING NOT WIDENING**: Alanchand 1,803,000 IRR/USD May 18 with page implying Sunday close 1,793K (conflicts with yesterday's logged 1,819K — Alanchand may have reset baseline); Bonbast last May 15 print 1,822K; remittance/Hawala 1,805K. Either Alanchand reset OR quote refresh — both point to ~1.05% Alanchand-Bonbast spread on Monday (1,803 vs 1,822), narrowing not widening. **Trigger 'source divergence persists beyond 5 sessions' (Day 4 of 5) likely to resolve UNTRIPPED tomorrow** unless Bonbast publishes wider print. **DXY 99.22 (-0.0% 1D, +1.1% 1M) — firm but not gapping**; EUR/USD 1.16 (-0.2%); USD/CNY 6.81 (+0.3%); USD/KRW ₩1,497.80 (+0.3% but still below ₩1,500); USD/BRL R$5.06 (+1.2% — Bolsonaro-Banco Master scandal); USD/TRY ₺45.57 (+0.1%). **Reversibility ASYMMETRIC**: crisis (~85%) reverses ONLY on (a) Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions AND (b) FII positive — neither imminent on Monday tape. RBI ~$150B deployable before import cover falls to 2013 levels = meaningful buffer.

recent_signals.headline: **C7 USD/INR ₹95.74 MAY 14 MORNING — RECORD CONTINUES, PACE SLOWED**: May 13 close ₹95.31 (4th straight session fresh closing record); RBI intervention sustaining defence at ₹95.50-75 zone. **₹96 trigger at Day 2 of 5-7 window — not breached.** At current ~5-7bps/day pace, ₹96 hits May 21-23 if Brent doesn't retrace. **A5 IRANIAN RIAL REVERSED MAY 13 — 1,842K (May 12) → 1,817K Hawala/Remittance series = -1.36%** (Alanchand primary). FIRST material reversal since May 6-10 stabilisation; 2M trigger ETA pushed beyond May 22-24. Caveat: Hawala/Remittance ≠ Bonbast — tomorrow's pipeline re-anchor on Bonbast. **G5 DXY 98.48 -0.0% 1D — DOLLAR FAILING TO BID ON CRISIS DAY**: -0.0% 1D, only +0.4% 1M. EM currency pressure driven by domestic + commodity-import pass-through, NOT global USD bid. USD/CNY 6.78 (-0.1%, PBOC stability into summit); USD/KRW ₩1,489.81 (-0.2% 1D) won steadying; **USD/BRL R$4.98 (+1.5% 1D) worst EM-FX (Bolsonaro-Banco Master scandal)**; USD/TRY ₺45.43 (+0.1%). RBI weekly reserves print due May 16 — last week was -$7.79bn; if this week >$10bn intervention sustainability moves from theoretical to operational concern. Reversibility ASYMMETRIC: requires Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions AND FII positive — neither imminent. RBI economists note India can deploy ~$150bn before import cover falls to 2013 levels.

recent_signals.headline: INR ₹95.54/USD MAY 12 INTRADAY = NEW RECORD — CLEANLY BREAKS ₹95.50 RBI DIRECT-INTERVENTION THRESHOLD; REGIME CHANGE. Trigger 'USD/INR crosses ₹95/USD' RE-BREACHED with fresh high after May 6 reversal. May 11 ₹95.0930 (intraday low ₹95.32 record); May 12 ₹95.54. CASCADE SELF-REINFORCING: Brent $105.58 → CAD widening → **FII single-day net sell ₹8,437.56 cr May 11 (highest since April 24)** → DII absorbed ₹5,939.65 cr (~70%) → **YTD CY26 FII outflow ₹2.06 lakh cr** (+₹14k cr in 2 sessions; already exceeds full-year 2025 ₹1.66 lakh cr by ₹40k cr) → RBI forex reserves -$7.79bn to $690.69bn week ended May 1 (active intervention not stopping). India 10Y G-Sec ~6.96-6.99%; new 10Y auction cut-off near 6.95%. **IRAN RIAL ACCELERATING APPROACH TO 2M TRIGGER**: 1,799K May 10 → 1,830K May 11 (+1.7%) → 1,853K May 12 (+1.26% d/d, +3.0% over 48h); official rate 1,311,203 = ~41% gap; 2M trigger hit by May 19-21 if linear extrapolation holds. PM Modi May 10-11 Hyderabad seven-appeal address (defer gold purchases for 1 year + defer foreign travel + cut fertilizer 50% + reduce edible oil/fuel + swadeshi) = first major Indian-government public engagement of cycle, targets FX pressure points (gold imports ~$50bn/yr + crude bill). FY27 GDP downgrade to 6.2% from 6.7% (global brokerage; 'historically large energy shock with asymmetric macro risks'). BMI pre-war ₹95-by-year-end forecast pulled forward 7 months. DXY 98.13 (+0.2% 1D); USD/CNY 6.79 (-0.1%); USD/KRW ₩1,488.28 (+1.9% 1D) on HMM Namu attack on Korea-flagged vessel + KOSPI -1.7%. Reversibility ASYMMETRIC: crisis component (~85%) reverses ONLY on (a) Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions AND (b) FII flow turn positive — neither imminent. Next test ₹96 within 5-7 sessions.
sources: Business Standard May 11 + May 12 live blogs (primary)
CNBC + Al Jazeera + DD News + Organiser (Modi address)
